# Privacy Filter Service — LLM Agent Reference

> Self-contained, complete, up-to-date API reference for agents.
> Last updated: 2026-05-12

---

## Project Overview

**Privacy Filter Service** is a local FastAPI service wrapping the [OpenAI Privacy Filter (OPF)](https://github.com/openai/privacy-filter) model. It detects and redacts Personally Identifiable Information (PII) from text.

**Primary use case**: Scan staged git files and commit messages for PII before committing. Blocks commits containing PII in tracked files, auto-redacts commit messages.

**Runtime**: Single uvicorn worker, asyncio.Lock-serialized inference on RTX 3090 GPU.

**Key properties**:
- All inference runs locally. No data leaves the machine.
- Fail-open: service down → warn + skip, never block developer workflow.
- Sanitized logging: no raw text in journald.
- 8 PII label types detected.

---

## Architecture

```
+------------------+        HTTP POST         +-------------------------+
|  git pre-commit  |  --------------------->  |  Privacy Filter Service |
|  git commit-msg  |  /redact  /redact/text   |  (FastAPI + OPF model)  |
+------------------+  /redact/batch           +-------------------------+
        |                                               |
        | blocks commit + writes patch                  | loads model at startup
        v                                               v
   redact-*.patch (in .git/privacy-filter/)      ~/.config/privacy-filter/config.toml
```

---

## API Endpoints

Base URL: `http://127.0.0.1:8765` (configurable)

All endpoints return `X-Request-ID` header (UUID4 generated if not provided).

---

### GET /health

Liveness and readiness probe.

**Request**: No body.

**Response 200** (`application/json`):
```json
{
  "ready": true,
  "device": "cuda",
  "uptime_s": 42.15,
  "version": "0.1.0"
}
```

**Response 503** (`application/json`):
```json
{
  "ready": false,
  "device": "unknown",
  "uptime_s": 0.0,
  "version": "0.1.0"
}
```

Returns 503 during model warmup (first ~20s after startup), 200 only after `OPF.redact()` smoke test passes.

---

### GET /model-info

Runtime model metadata. Does NOT expose `model_path` (security: avoids leaking filesystem layout).

**Request**: No body.

**Response 200** (`application/json`):
```json
{
  "device": "cuda",
  "labels": [
    "account_number",
    "private_address",
    "private_email",
    "private_person",
    "private_phone",
    "private_url",
    "private_date",
    "secret"
  ],
  "output_mode": "typed",
  "decode_mode": "viterbi",
  "version": "0.1.0"
}
```

**Response 503**: Engine not ready.

---

### POST /redact

Redact PII from a single text string. Returns full structured result with detected spans.

**Request** (`application/json`):
```json
{
  "text": "Email me at alice@example.com or call 555-123-4567"
}
```

**Response 200** (`application/json`):
```json
{
  "text": "Email me at alice@example.com or call 555-123-4567",
  "redacted_text": "Email me at <PRIVATE_EMAIL> or call <PRIVATE_PHONE>",
  "detected_spans": [
    {
      "label": "private_email",
      "start": 12,
      "end": 28,
      "text": "alice@example.com",
      "placeholder": "<PRIVATE_EMAIL>"
    },
    {
      "label": "private_phone",
      "start": 37,
      "end": 49,
      "text": "555-123-4567",
      "placeholder": "<PRIVATE_PHONE>"
    }
  ],
  "summary": {
    "output_mode": "typed",
    "span_count": 2,
    "by_label": {
      "private_email": 1,
      "private_phone": 1
    },
    "decoded_mismatch": false
  },
  "schema_version": 1,
  "warning": null
}
```

**Response 413**: Text exceeds `max_file_bytes` (default 262144 bytes).

**Response 503**: Engine not ready.

---

### POST /redact/text

Redact PII and return only the redacted text as plain text. Optimized for hook use (simpler parsing).

**Request** (`application/json`):
```json
{
  "text": "Alice was born on 1990-01-02"
}
```

**Response 200** (`text/plain`):
```
<PRIVATE_PERSON> was born on <PRIVATE_DATE>
```

**Response 413**: Text exceeds `max_file_bytes`.

**Response 503**: Engine not ready.

---

### POST /redact/batch

Redact PII from multiple texts in a single request. Returns results in the same order as input.

**Request** (`application/json`):
```json
{
  "texts": [
    "Hello world",
    "alice@example.com",
    "555-123-4567"
  ]
}
```

**Response 200** (`application/json`):
```json
[
  {
    "text": "Hello world",
    "redacted_text": "Hello world",
    "detected_spans": [],
    "summary": {
      "output_mode": "typed",
      "span_count": 0,
      "by_label": {},
      "decoded_mismatch": false
    },
    "schema_version": 1,
    "warning": null
  },
  {
    "text": "alice@example.com",
    "redacted_text": "<PRIVATE_EMAIL>",
    "detected_spans": [
      {
        "label": "private_email",
        "start": 0,
        "end": 17,
        "text": "alice@example.com",
        "placeholder": "<PRIVATE_EMAIL>"
      }
    ],
    "summary": {
      "output_mode": "typed",
      "span_count": 1,
      "by_label": {"private_email": 1},
      "decoded_mismatch": false
    },
    "schema_version": 1,
    "warning": null
  },
  {
    "text": "555-123-4567",
    "redacted_text": "<PRIVATE_PHONE>",
    "detected_spans": [
      {
        "label": "private_phone",
        "start": 0,
        "end": 12,
        "text": "555-123-4567",
        "placeholder": "<PRIVATE_PHONE>"
      }
    ],
    "summary": {
      "output_mode": "typed",
      "span_count": 1,
      "by_label": {"private_phone": 1},
      "decoded_mismatch": false
    },
    "schema_version": 1,
    "warning": null
  }
]
```

**Limits**:
- Max 100 texts per batch (Pydantic `max_length=100`)
- Each text max `max_file_bytes` bytes
- Returns 413 with `{"detail":"text at index N exceeds maximum allowed size"}` if any text oversized
- Returns 422 if batch exceeds 100 items (Pydantic validation)

**Response 503**: Engine not ready.

---

## Pydantic Models (Request/Response)

All models use Pydantic v2.

### RedactRequest
```python
class RedactRequest(BaseModel):
    text: str  # The text to redact
```

### RedactBatchRequest
```python
class RedactBatchRequest(BaseModel):
    texts: list[str] = Field(..., max_length=100)  # Up to 100 texts
```

### DetectedSpan
```python
class DetectedSpan(BaseModel):
    label: str       # One of the 8 PII labels
    start: int       # Character start index (inclusive)
    end: int         # Character end index (exclusive)
    text: str        # Original text of the span
    placeholder: str # Replacement placeholder, e.g. "<PRIVATE_EMAIL>"
```

### RedactionSummary
```python
class RedactionSummary(BaseModel):
    output_mode: str           # "typed" or "redacted"
    span_count: int            # Total detected spans
    by_label: dict[str, int]   # Count per label, e.g. {"private_email": 2}
    decoded_mismatch: bool = False  # Internal: viterbi vs argmax disagreement
```

### RedactionResult
```python
class RedactionResult(BaseModel):
    text: str                          # Original input text
    redacted_text: str                 # Text with PII replaced by placeholders
    detected_spans: list[DetectedSpan] # All detected PII spans
    summary: RedactionSummary          # Compact summary
    schema_version: int = 1            # Always 1
    warning: str | None = None         # Optional warning (e.g. decode mismatch)
```

### HealthResponse
```python
class HealthResponse(BaseModel):
    ready: bool     # True only after model warmup completes
    device: str     # "cuda" or "cpu"
    uptime_s: float # Seconds since service started
    version: str    # "0.1.0"
```

### ModelInfoResponse
```python
class ModelInfoResponse(BaseModel):
    device: str          # "cuda" or "cpu"
    labels: list[str]    # All 8 PII label names
    output_mode: str     # "typed" or "redacted"
    decode_mode: str     # "viterbi" or "argmax"
    version: str         # "0.1.0"
    # NOTE: model_path and checkpoint are intentionally excluded (security)
```

---

## PII Label Taxonomy

The model detects and redacts 8 categories:

| Label | Description | Example |
|-------|-------------|---------|
| `account_number` | Bank/account numbers | `1234567890` |
| `private_address` | Physical addresses | `123 Main St, Springfield` |
| `private_email` | Email addresses | `alice@example.com` |
| `private_person` | Person names | `Alice Smith` |
| `private_phone` | Phone numbers | `555-123-4567` |
| `private_url` | URLs | `https://example.com/profile` |
| `private_date` | Dates of birth, etc. | `1990-01-02` |
| `secret` | API keys, passwords | `sk-abc123...` |

---

## Configuration

### Config file location
`~/.config/privacy-filter/config.toml`

Overridable via `PRIVACY_FILTER_CONFIG` env var.

### TOML schema

```toml
[service]
host = "0.0.0.0"           # Listen address
port = 8765                 # Listen port (1-65535)
device = "cuda"             # "cuda" or "cpu"
output_mode = "typed"       # "typed" (with labels) or "redacted" (collapsed)
decode_mode = "viterbi"     # "viterbi" or "argmax"
model_path = ""             # REQUIRED — path to OPF checkpoint dir
log_level = "INFO"          # Python logging level

[hook]
base_url = "http://127.0.0.1:8765"  # Service URL hooks call
request_timeout_s = 5.0             # Hook HTTP timeout (1-60 seconds)
max_file_bytes = 262144             # Max file size hooks send (<= 1048576)
max_inflight_warns_per_5min = 1     # Rate limit for repeated warnings
```

### Environment variable overrides

All config keys can be overridden by env vars (loaded after TOML):

| Env var | Maps to | Type |
|---------|---------|------|
| `PRIVACY_FILTER_LISTEN_HOST` | `service.host` | str |
| `PRIVACY_FILTER_LISTEN_PORT` | `service.port` | int |
| `PRIVACY_FILTER_DEVICE` | `service.device` | str |
| `PRIVACY_FILTER_OUTPUT_MODE` | `service.output_mode` | str |
| `PRIVACY_FILTER_DECODE_MODE` | `service.decode_mode` | str |
| `PRIVACY_FILTER_MODEL_PATH` | `service.model_path` | str |
| `PRIVACY_FILTER_LOG_LEVEL` | `service.log_level` | str |
| `PRIVACY_FILTER_URL` | `hook.base_url` | str |
| `PRIVACY_FILTER_TIMEOUT_S` | `hook.request_timeout_s` | float |
| `PRIVACY_FILTER_MAX_FILE_BYTES` | `hook.max_file_bytes` | int |
| `PRIVACY_FILTER_MAX_INFLIGHT_WARNS` | `hook.max_inflight_warns_per_5min` | int |
| `OPF_CHECKPOINT` | `service.model_path` (fallback) | str |

Resolution order: TOML → `PRIVACY_FILTER_*` env → `OPF_CHECKPOINT` fallback.

### Validation rules

- `port`: 1–65535
- `device`: `cuda` or `cpu`
- `output_mode`: `typed` or `redacted`
- `decode_mode`: `viterbi` or `argmax`
- `max_file_bytes`: <= 1048576 (1 MB)
- `request_timeout_s`: 1–60
- `model_path`: **required** (no silent fallback)
- Crashes with `SystemExit` on invalid config (fail-fast)

---

## OPF Engine

### OPFEngine class

Async singleton wrapper around `opf.OPF`. Key behaviors:

- **Lazy initialization**: Constructor does NOT load the model. `warmup()` loads it.
- **asyncio.Lock serialization**: All `redact()` calls acquire the lock. Model is not validated for concurrency.
- **CUDA validation**: If `device="cuda"` and `torch.cuda.is_available()` is False, `warmup()` raises `RuntimeError` (fail-fast at startup, not per-request).
- **Singleton pattern**: Module-level `_engine` variable + `get_engine()` FastAPI dependency.

### Constructor

```python
OPFEngine(service_settings: ServiceConfig)
# Attributes: device, output_mode, decode_mode, model_path, ready (bool)
```

### Methods

```python
async warmup() -> None
# Loads OPF model, runs smoke test redact("test"), sets ready=True
# Raises RuntimeError if CUDA unavailable when device="cuda"

async redact(text: str) -> RedactionResult
# Acquires lock, calls OPF.redact(), converts to Pydantic model
# Calls warmup() first if not ready

async redact_batch(texts: list[str]) -> list[RedactionResult]
# Iterates texts under lock (one-at-a-time), returns results in order
# Calls warmup() first if not ready
```

### get_engine() dependency

```python
async def get_engine(settings: Annotated[Settings, Depends(load_settings)]) -> OPFEngine
```

Returns singleton engine, warming it lazily on first use. Used as FastAPI `Depends()`.

---

## Git Hooks

### Pre-commit hook (`hooks/pre-commit`)

**Behavior**:
1. Checks `PRIVACY_FILTER_SKIP=1` → exit 0
2. Checks service health (`/health`) → fail-open if down
3. Lists fully-staged files (`git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACM`)
4. Detects partial staging → abort with message
5. For each text file (UTF-8, not binary/symlink/submodule/LFS/oversized):
   - Extracts staged content via `git show :<path>`
   - POSTs to `/redact`
   - If spans detected: saves orig + redacted to `.git/privacy-filter/`
6. If any PII found:
   - Generates unified diff patch at `.git/privacy-filter/redact-<timestamp>-<pid>.patch`
   - Validates patch via `git apply --check`
   - Blocks commit (exit 1) with apply instructions
7. Otherwise: exit 0

**File filtering** (skipped files):
- Binary (detected via `git diff --cached --numstat` showing `-\t-`)
- Symlinks
- Git submodules (mode `160000`)
- LFS pointer files (content starts with `version https://git-lfs.github.com/spec/v1`)
- Files > `max_file_bytes` (default 256 KB)
- Non-UTF-8 encoding (detected via `file --mime-encoding`)
- Files with `binary` git attribute

**Patch format**: Standard unified diff compatible with `git apply --index`.

### Commit-msg hook (`hooks/commit-msg`)

**Behavior**:
1. Checks `PRIVACY_FILTER_SKIP=1` → exit 0
2. Checks service health → fail-open if down
3. Reads message file (`$1`), splits into 3 zones:
   - **Body**: user-written lines (redacted)
   - **Comments**: lines starting with `#` (preserved untouched)
   - **Verbose section**: lines after `------ >8 ------` (preserved untouched)
4. POSTs body to `/redact/text`
5. If redacted text differs from original:
   - Reconstructs file: redacted body + original comments + verbose section
   - Preserves BOM if present
   - Writes back to message file
   - Prints span count + by_label summary to stderr
6. **Always exits 0** — commit-msg never blocks

### Bypass mechanisms

- `PRIVACY_FILTER_SKIP=1` env var → skip all hooks
- `git commit --no-verify` → bypass all git hooks
- `PRIVACY_FILTER_URL=http://invalid:1` → service down → fail-open

### Hook shared library (`hooks/_lib.sh`)

Key functions:

| Function | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `pf_url()` | Returns `PRIVACY_FILTER_URL` or default `http://127.0.0.1:8765` |
| `pf_skip_active()` | Returns 0 if `PRIVACY_FILTER_SKIP=1` |
| `pf_is_text_file <path>` | Returns 0 if file is UTF-8 text, not binary/symlink/submodule/LFS |
| `pf_is_lfs_pointer <path>` | Returns 0 if file is a Git LFS pointer |
| `pf_too_large <bytes>` | Returns 0 if size > `max_file_bytes` |
| `pf_post_json <endpoint> <json>` | POST JSON to service, body on stdout, HTTP code on stderr |
| `pf_warn_once <key> <msg>` | Rate-limited warning (once per 5 min per key) |
| `pf_fail_open <reason>` | Print warning + exit 0 |
| `pf_ensure_dir` | Create `.git/privacy-filter/` with 0700 perms |
| `pf_service_ready` | Check `/health` returns `ready=true` |
| `pf_cleanup_old_patches` | Delete patches older than 24 hours |

---

## Error Handling

| Status | Meaning | When |
|--------|---------|------|
| 200 | Success | Normal operation |
| 413 | Payload too large | Text exceeds `max_file_bytes` or batch > 100 items |
| 422 | Validation error | Invalid JSON, missing `text` field, batch > 100 (Pydantic) |
| 503 | Service unavailable | Engine not ready (during warmup or startup failure) |

**Error responses** never include raw input text (security).

**Hook fail-open matrix**:
- Connection refused → warn + exit 0
- Timeout → warn + exit 0
- HTTP 5xx → warn + exit 0
- Malformed JSON response → warn + exit 0
- HTTP 413 (oversized) → skip file, continue

---

## Logging

Structured JSON to stdout. Fields: `timestamp`, `level`, `logger`, `request_id`, `msg`, `span_count`, `duration_ms`.

**Redacted fields** (replaced with `<REDACTED>` in any log record):
`text`, `message`, `content`, `payload`, `redacted_text`, `detected_spans`, `placeholder`

**Uvicorn access log**: Disabled (handlers cleared, propagate=False, level > CRITICAL).

**Exception tracebacks**: Replaced with `<REDACTED>` to prevent body leakage.

---

## Installation

### Prerequisites

- Python 3.10+
- [uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/) package manager
- OPF installed editable from `/home/chriswang/project/docker/privacy-filter`
- Model checkpoint at `/mnt/LLM/OpenAI/privacy_filter`
- CUDA-capable GPU (optional; CPU fallback works)

### Install service

```bash
cd ~/project/docker/privacy-filter-service
bash install/install-service.sh
```

This: creates venv, installs deps, deploys config.toml + env, installs systemd user unit, starts service, waits for health.

### Install hooks

```bash
bash install/install-hooks.sh
```

This: copies hooks to `~/.config/git/hooks/`, sets `git config --global core.hooksPath`.

Use `--force` to override existing `core.hooksPath`.

### Uninstall

```bash
bash install/uninstall.sh
```

Preserves `~/.config/privacy-filter/config.toml` and `env`.

---

## Systemd Operation

```bash
systemctl --user start privacy-filter
systemctl --user stop privacy-filter
systemctl --user restart privacy-filter
systemctl --user status privacy-filter
journalctl --user -u privacy-filter -f
```

Unit file: `~/.config/systemd/user/privacy-filter.service`

Uses `%h` specifier (user home), `EnvironmentFile=-%h/.config/privacy-filter/env` (optional), `Restart=on-failure`, `RestartSec=5s`.

For boot-time availability: `loginctl enable-linger $USER`

---

## Testing

### Pytest (service)

```bash
cd ~/project/docker/privacy-filter-service
uv run pytest -q              # 68 tests, no GPU required
uv run pytest -q -m gpu       # 1 test, requires GPU
```

### Integration tests (hooks)

```bash
bash tests/integration/run_all.sh   # 13 tests in isolated /tmp repos
```

Tests: clean commit, PII commit + patch apply, partial staging abort, binary skip, oversize skip, LFS pointer skip, commit-msg redact/clean/comment preservation, service-down fail-open, skip env bypass, no-verify bypass, concurrent commits, installer collision/force/uninstall.

### End-to-end smoke

```bash
bash tests/e2e/smoke.sh   # 7 scenarios, idempotent, <60s
```

Full lifecycle: install → hook tests → journal leak check → uninstall → cleanup.

---

## Source Files

### Project structure

```
privacy-filter-service/
├── pyproject.toml                          # Build config, deps, entry point
├── config.toml.example                     # Config template
├── README.md                               # User documentation
├── config/
│   └── env.example                         # Environment variable template
├── src/privacy_filter_service/
│   ├── __init__.py                         # Package init
│   ├── app.py                              # FastAPI factory, routes, entrypoint
│   ├── config.py                           # TOML + env config loader
│   ├── logging_setup.py                    # Sanitized JSON logging + middleware
│   ├── models.py                           # Pydantic request/response models
│   └── opf_engine.py                       # Async OPF wrapper with Lock
├── hooks/
│   ├── _lib.sh                             # Shared bash library
│   ├── pre-commit                          # Pre-commit hook (scan + patch)
│   └── commit-msg                          # Commit-msg hook (auto-redact)
├── install/
│   ├── install-service.sh                  # Service installer
│   ├── install-hooks.sh                    # Hook installer
│   └── uninstall.sh                        # Uninstaller
├── systemd/
│   └── privacy-filter.service              # systemd user unit
├── tests/
│   ├── conftest.py                         # Shared fixtures
│   ├── test_config.py                      # Config loader tests
│   ├── test_models.py                      # Pydantic model tests
│   ├── test_logging.py                     # Logging redaction tests
│   ├── test_opf_engine.py                  # Engine wrapper tests
│   ├── test_health.py                      # /health + /model-info tests
│   ├── test_redact.py                      # /redact + /redact/text tests
│   ├── test_batch.py                       # /redact/batch tests
│   ├── integration/                        # Bash integration tests
│   │   ├── run_all.sh                      # Test runner
│   │   ├── common.sh                       # Shared helpers
│   │   ├── mock_service.py                 # Mock HTTP service
│   │   └── test_*.sh                       # Individual test scripts
│   └── e2e/
│       └── smoke.sh                        # End-to-end smoke test
└── docs/
    └── llms-full.txt                       # This file
```

### Key source: app.py

```python
"""FastAPI application factory with lifespan-managed OPF engine."""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import time
from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
from typing import AsyncIterator

import uvicorn
from fastapi import Depends, FastAPI, Request, Response
from fastapi.responses import PlainTextResponse

from privacy_filter_service.config import Settings, load_settings
from privacy_filter_service.logging_setup import configure_logging, request_id_middleware
from privacy_filter_service.models import (
    LABELS,
    HealthResponse,
    ModelInfoResponse,
    RedactBatchRequest,
    RedactRequest,
)
from privacy_filter_service.opf_engine import OPFEngine

log = logging.getLogger(__name__)

MAX_BATCH_SIZE = 100


def _get_engine(request: Request) -> OPFEngine | None:
    engine: OPFEngine | None = getattr(request.app.state, "engine", None)
    return engine


@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI) -> AsyncIterator[None]:
    """Create engine, warm up model, tear down on shutdown."""
    settings: Settings = app.state.settings
    engine = OPFEngine(settings.service)
    app.state.engine = engine
    app.state.start_time = time.monotonic()
    log.info("warming up OPF engine (device=%s)", engine.device)
    await engine.warmup()
    log.info("OPF engine ready (device=%s)", engine.device)
    yield
    app.state.engine = None


def create_app(settings: Settings | None = None) -> FastAPI:
    """Build and return a configured FastAPI instance."""
    if settings is None:
        settings = load_settings()

    app = FastAPI(
        title="Privacy Filter Service",
        version="0.1.0",
        lifespan=lifespan,
        docs_url=None,
        redoc_url=None,
    )
    app.state.settings = settings
    app.add_middleware(request_id_middleware)

    _register_routes(app)
    return app


def _register_routes(app: FastAPI) -> None:
    """Attach all endpoint handlers to *app*."""

    @app.get("/health", response_model=HealthResponse)
    async def health(request: Request) -> HealthResponse | Response:
        engine: OPFEngine | None = getattr(request.app.state, "engine", None)
        if engine is None or not engine.ready:
            return Response(
                content=HealthResponse(
                    ready=False, device="unknown", uptime_s=0.0, version="0.1.0"
                ).model_dump_json(),
                status_code=503,
                media_type="application/json",
            )
        uptime = time.monotonic() - getattr(request.app.state, "start_time", 0)
        return HealthResponse(
            ready=True,
            device=engine.device,
            uptime_s=round(uptime, 2),
            version="0.1.0",
        )

    @app.get("/model-info", response_model=ModelInfoResponse)
    async def model_info(
        request: Request,
        engine: OPFEngine | None = Depends(_get_engine),
    ) -> ModelInfoResponse | Response:
        if engine is None or not engine.ready:
            return Response(status_code=503, content='{"detail":"engine not ready"}')
        return ModelInfoResponse(
            device=engine.device,
            labels=list(LABELS),
            output_mode=engine.output_mode,
            decode_mode=engine.decode_mode,
            version="0.1.0",
        )

    @app.post("/redact")
    async def redact(
        body: RedactRequest,
        request: Request,
        engine: OPFEngine | None = Depends(_get_engine),
    ) -> Response:
        if engine is None or not engine.ready:
            return Response(status_code=503, content='{"detail":"engine not ready"}')
        settings: Settings = request.app.state.settings
        if len(body.text.encode("utf-8")) > settings.hook.max_file_bytes:
            return Response(
                status_code=413,
                content='{"detail":"text exceeds maximum allowed size"}',
                media_type="application/json",
            )
        t0 = time.monotonic()
        result = await engine.redact(body.text)
        duration_ms = round((time.monotonic() - t0) * 1000, 2)
        log.info(
            "redact request",
            extra={
                "endpoint": "/redact",
                "text_len": len(body.text),
                "span_count": result.summary.span_count,
                "duration_ms": duration_ms,
            },
        )
        return Response(
            content=result.model_dump_json(),
            media_type="application/json",
        )

    @app.post("/redact/text")
    async def redact_text(
        body: RedactRequest,
        request: Request,
        engine: OPFEngine | None = Depends(_get_engine),
    ) -> Response:
        if engine is None or not engine.ready:
            return Response(status_code=503, content='{"detail":"engine not ready"}')
        settings: Settings = request.app.state.settings
        if len(body.text.encode("utf-8")) > settings.hook.max_file_bytes:
            return Response(
                status_code=413,
                content='{"detail":"text exceeds maximum allowed size"}',
                media_type="application/json",
            )
        t0 = time.monotonic()
        result = await engine.redact(body.text)
        duration_ms = round((time.monotonic() - t0) * 1000, 2)
        log.info(
            "redact/text request",
            extra={
                "endpoint": "/redact/text",
                "text_len": len(body.text),
                "span_count": result.summary.span_count,
                "duration_ms": duration_ms,
            },
        )
        return PlainTextResponse(result.redacted_text)

    @app.post("/redact/batch")
    async def redact_batch(
        body: RedactBatchRequest,
        request: Request,
        engine: OPFEngine | None = Depends(_get_engine),
    ) -> Response:
        if engine is None or not engine.ready:
            return Response(status_code=503, content='{"detail":"engine not ready"}')
        settings: Settings = request.app.state.settings
        if len(body.texts) > MAX_BATCH_SIZE:
            return Response(
                status_code=413,
                content='{"detail":f"batch size {len(body.texts)} exceeds limit of {MAX_BATCH_SIZE}"}',
                media_type="application/json",
            )
        for idx, text in enumerate(body.texts):
            if len(text.encode("utf-8")) > settings.hook.max_file_bytes:
                return Response(
                    status_code=413,
                    content=f'{{"detail":"text at index {idx} exceeds maximum allowed size"}}',
                    media_type="application/json",
                )
        t0 = time.monotonic()
        results = await engine.redact_batch(body.texts)
        duration_ms = round((time.monotonic() - t0) * 1000, 2)
        total_spans = sum(r.summary.span_count for r in results)
        log.info(
            "redact/batch request",
            extra={
                "endpoint": "/redact/batch",
                "text_len": sum(len(t) for t in body.texts),
                "span_count": total_spans,
                "duration_ms": duration_ms,
            },
        )
        import json

        return Response(
            content=json.dumps([r.model_dump() for r in results]),
            media_type="application/json",
        )


def main() -> None:
    """CLI entrypoint — configure logging and start uvicorn."""
    settings = load_settings()
    configure_logging(settings.service.log_level)
    app = create_app(settings)
    uvicorn.run(
        app,
        host=settings.service.host,
        port=settings.service.port,
        workers=1,
        access_log=False,
    )
```

### Key source: config.py

```python
"""Pydantic-based configuration loader with TOML + env-var overlay."""

from __future__ import annotations

import os
import sys
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Literal

try:
    import tomllib
except ModuleNotFoundError:
    import tomli as tomllib  # type: ignore[no-redef]

from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, ValidationError

OPF_CHECKPOINT_ENV = "OPF_CHECKPOINT"
PRIVACY_FILTER_CONFIG_ENV = "PRIVACY_FILTER_CONFIG"

_ENV_OVERRIDES: dict[str, str] = {
    "PRIVACY_FILTER_LISTEN_HOST": "service.host",
    "PRIVACY_FILTER_LISTEN_PORT": "service.port",
    "PRIVACY_FILTER_DEVICE": "service.device",
    "PRIVACY_FILTER_OUTPUT_MODE": "service.output_mode",
    "PRIVACY_FILTER_DECODE_MODE": "service.decode_mode",
    "PRIVACY_FILTER_MODEL_PATH": "service.model_path",
    "PRIVACY_FILTER_LOG_LEVEL": "service.log_level",
    "PRIVACY_FILTER_URL": "hook.base_url",
    "PRIVACY_FILTER_TIMEOUT_S": "hook.request_timeout_s",
    "PRIVACY_FILTER_MAX_FILE_BYTES": "hook.max_file_bytes",
    "PRIVACY_FILTER_MAX_INFLIGHT_WARNS": "hook.max_inflight_warns_per_5min",
}


def _deep_set(data: dict, dotted: str, value: object) -> None:
    parts = dotted.split(".")
    current = data
    for part in parts[:-1]:
        current = current.setdefault(part, {})
    current[parts[-1]] = value


def _apply_env_overlays(raw: dict) -> dict:
    for env_var, dotted_key in _ENV_OVERRIDES.items():
        val = os.environ.get(env_var)
        if val is None:
            continue
        if dotted_key in (
            "service.port",
            "hook.request_timeout_s",
            "hook.max_file_bytes",
            "hook.max_inflight_warns_per_5min",
        ):
            try:
                val = int(val)
            except ValueError:
                try:
                    val = float(val)
                except ValueError:
                    pass
        _deep_set(raw, dotted_key, val)
    return raw


class ServiceConfig(BaseModel):
    """Configuration for the privacy-filter inference service."""

    host: str = "0.0.0.0"
    port: int = Field(default=8765, ge=1, le=65535)
    device: Literal["cuda", "cpu"] = "cuda"
    output_mode: Literal["typed", "redacted"] = "typed"
    decode_mode: Literal["viterbi", "argmax"] = "viterbi"
    model_path: str
    log_level: str = "INFO"


class HookConfig(BaseModel):
    """Configuration for the Git pre-receive hook client."""

    base_url: str = "http://127.0.0.1:8765"
    request_timeout_s: float = Field(default=5.0, ge=1, le=60)
    max_file_bytes: int = Field(default=262144, le=1_048_576)
    max_inflight_warns_per_5min: int = 1


class Settings(BaseModel):
    """Top-level application settings."""

    service: ServiceConfig
    hook: HookConfig = Field(default_factory=HookConfig)


def load_settings(
    config_path: str | Path | None = None,
    config_text: str | None = None,
) -> Settings:
    """Load TOML config, overlay env vars, validate, and return Settings.

    Resolution order (later wins):
      1. TOML file (~/.config/privacy-filter/config.toml or PRIVACY_FILTER_CONFIG)
      2. PRIVACY_FILTER_* env var overrides
      3. OPF_CHECKPOINT env -> service.model_path (only if TOML didn't set it)

    Crashes with SystemExit on invalid or missing config.
    """
    if config_text is not None:
        raw: dict = tomllib.loads(config_text)
    else:
        if config_path is None:
            config_path = os.environ.get(
                PRIVACY_FILTER_CONFIG_ENV,
                Path.home() / ".config" / "privacy-filter" / "config.toml",
            )
        config_path = Path(config_path)
        if not config_path.is_file():
            print(
                f"[FATAL] Config file not found: {config_path}",
                file=sys.stderr,
            )
            sys.exit(1)
        raw = tomllib.loads(config_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))

    raw = _apply_env_overlays(raw)

    svc = raw.setdefault("service", {})
    if "model_path" not in svc:
        checkpoint = os.environ.get(OPF_CHECKPOINT_ENV)
        if checkpoint:
            svc["model_path"] = checkpoint

    try:
        return Settings.model_validate(raw)
    except ValidationError as exc:
        print("[FATAL] Invalid configuration:", file=sys.stderr)
        print(exc, file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
```

### Key source: opf_engine.py

```python
"""Async singleton wrapper for the local OPF redaction engine."""

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
from typing import Annotated, Literal

from fastapi import Depends
from opf._api import OPF, RedactionResult as OPFRedactionResult

from privacy_filter_service.config import ServiceConfig, Settings, load_settings
from privacy_filter_service.models import RedactionResult


class OPFEngine:
    """Serialize access to a single OPF instance."""

    def __init__(self, service_settings: ServiceConfig) -> None:
        self.device: Literal["cuda", "cpu"] = service_settings.device
        self.output_mode: Literal["typed", "redacted"] = service_settings.output_mode
        self.decode_mode: Literal["viterbi", "argmax"] = service_settings.decode_mode
        self.model_path: str = service_settings.model_path
        self.ready: bool = False
        self._opf: OPF | None = None
        self._lock: asyncio.Lock = asyncio.Lock()

    @property
    def lock(self) -> asyncio.Lock:
        return self._lock

    async def warmup(self) -> None:
        """Instantiate OPF once and verify the configured device works."""
        async with self._lock:
            if self.ready:
                return
            self._validate_device()
            opf = OPF(
                model=self.model_path,
                device=self.device,
                output_mode=self.output_mode,
                decode_mode=self.decode_mode,
            )
            smoke: str | OPFRedactionResult = opf.redact("test")
            if isinstance(smoke, str):
                raise TypeError("OPF.redact() returned text-only output during warmup")
            self._opf = opf
            self.ready = True

    async def redact(self, text: str) -> RedactionResult:
        await self.warmup()
        async with self._lock:
            opf = self._require_opf()
            result: str | OPFRedactionResult = opf.redact(text)
            if isinstance(result, str):
                raise TypeError("OPF.redact() returned text-only output")
            return RedactionResult.model_validate(result.to_dict())

    async def redact_batch(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[RedactionResult]:
        await self.warmup()
        async with self._lock:
            opf = self._require_opf()
            results: list[RedactionResult] = []
            for text in texts:
                result: str | OPFRedactionResult = opf.redact(text)
                if isinstance(result, str):
                    raise TypeError("OPF.redact() returned text-only output")
                results.append(RedactionResult.model_validate(result.to_dict()))
            return results

    def _require_opf(self) -> OPF:
        if self._opf is None:
            raise RuntimeError("OPF engine not initialized")
        return self._opf

    def _validate_device(self) -> None:
        if self.device != "cuda":
            return
        import torch

        if not torch.cuda.is_available():
            raise RuntimeError("CUDA requested for OPFEngine, but torch.cuda.is_available() is False")


_engine: OPFEngine | None = None


async def get_engine(settings: Annotated[Settings, Depends(load_settings)]) -> OPFEngine:
    """Return the singleton engine instance, warming it lazily on first use."""
    global _engine
    if _engine is None:
        _engine = OPFEngine(settings.service)
    if not _engine.ready:
        await _engine.warmup()
    return _engine
```

### Key source: models.py

```python
"""Pydantic v2 models for the privacy-filter-service API."""

from __future__ import annotations

from pydantic import BaseModel, Field


# Label taxonomy

LABELS: tuple[str, ...] = (
    "account_number",
    "private_address",
    "private_email",
    "private_person",
    "private_phone",
    "private_url",
    "private_date",
    "secret",
)

assert len(LABELS) == 8


# Request models

class RedactRequest(BaseModel):
    """Single-text redaction request."""
    text: str


class RedactBatchRequest(BaseModel):
    """Batch redaction request (up to 100 texts)."""
    texts: list[str] = Field(..., max_length=100)


# Response domain models

class DetectedSpan(BaseModel):
    """One detected character span with label and placeholder."""
    label: str
    start: int
    end: int
    text: str
    placeholder: str


class RedactionSummary(BaseModel):
    """Compact summary of a redaction pass."""
    output_mode: str
    span_count: int
    by_label: dict[str, int]
    decoded_mismatch: bool = False


class RedactionResult(BaseModel):
    """Structured result returned by the public OPF redaction API."""
    text: str
    redacted_text: str
    detected_spans: list[DetectedSpan]
    summary: RedactionSummary
    schema_version: int = 1
    warning: str | None = None


# Operational models

class HealthResponse(BaseModel):
    """Liveness / readiness probe response."""
    ready: bool
    device: str
    uptime_s: float
    version: str


class ModelInfoResponse(BaseModel):
    """Runtime model metadata exposed to callers.
    Intentionally omits model_path and checkpoint (security)."""
    device: str
    labels: list[str]
    output_mode: str
    decode_mode: str
    version: str
```

### Key source: logging_setup.py

```python
"""Sanitized logging setup — never logs raw text."""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import logging
import sys
import uuid
from contextvars import ContextVar
from typing import Any, Callable, Optional

_request_id: ContextVar[str] = ContextVar("request_id", default="-")

_REDACT_KEYS = frozenset(
    {"text", "message", "content", "payload", "redacted_text", "detected_spans", "placeholder"}
)

_FORMATTER_SKIP_KEYS = frozenset({
    "name", "msg", "args", "created", "relativeCreated",
    "exc_info", "exc_text", "stack_info", "lineno", "funcName",
    "pathname", "filename", "module", "thread", "threadName",
    "process", "processName", "levelname", "levelno", "message",
    "msecs", "taskName",
})


class RedactFilter(logging.Filter):
    """Strips sensitive fields from LogRecord attributes and extra dict."""
    def filter(self, record: logging.LogRecord) -> bool:
        for key in list(record.__dict__.keys()):
            if key in _REDACT_KEYS:
                setattr(record, key, "<REDACTED>")
        return True


class _SafeJSONFormatter(logging.Formatter):
    """Emit one JSON object per line with guaranteed redaction."""
    def format(self, record: logging.LogRecord) -> str:
        payload: dict[str, Any] = {
            "timestamp": self.formatTime(record, self.datefmt),
            "level": record.levelname,
            "logger": record.name,
            "request_id": _request_id.get("-"),
            "msg": record.getMessage(),
        }
        safe_extra_keys = {
            k: v for k, v in record.__dict__.items()
            if k not in _REDACT_KEYS and k not in _FORMATTER_SKIP_KEYS
        }
        payload["span_count"] = safe_extra_keys.pop("span_count", 0)
        payload["duration_ms"] = safe_extra_keys.pop("duration_ms", 0)
        payload.update(safe_extra_keys)
        for key in _REDACT_KEYS:
            payload.pop(key, None)
        if record.exc_info and not record.exc_text:
            record.exc_text = self.formatException(record.exc_info)
        if record.exc_text:
            payload["exception"] = "<REDACTED>"
        return json.dumps(payload, default=str)


def configure_logging(level: str = "INFO", *, stream: Optional[Any] = None) -> None:
    """Set up structured JSON logging."""
    root = logging.getLogger()
    root.handlers.clear()
    handler = logging.StreamHandler(stream or sys.stdout)
    handler.setFormatter(_SafeJSONFormatter(datefmt="%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%fZ"))
    handler.addFilter(RedactFilter())
    root.setLevel(getattr(logging, level.upper(), logging.INFO))
    root.addHandler(handler)
    logging.getLogger("uvicorn.access").handlers.clear()
    logging.getLogger("uvicorn.access").propagate = False
    logging.getLogger("uvicorn.access").setLevel(logging.CRITICAL + 1)
    logging.getLogger("uvicorn.error").addFilter(RedactFilter())


def request_id_middleware(app: Any) -> Callable:
    """ASGI middleware that injects X-Request-ID into every request."""
    from starlette.middleware.base import BaseHTTPMiddleware
    from starlette.requests import Request
    from starlette.responses import Response

    class _Middleware(BaseHTTPMiddleware):
        async def dispatch(self, request: Request, call_next: Callable) -> Response:
            rid = request.headers.get("X-Request-ID", str(uuid.uuid4()))
            token = _request_id.set(rid)
            try:
                response = await call_next(request)
            finally:
                _request_id.reset(token)
            response.headers["X-Request-ID"] = rid
            return response

    return _Middleware(app)
```

---

## Quick Reference

### curl examples

```bash
# Health check
curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:8765/health | jq .

# Model info
curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:8765/model-info | jq .

# Single redact (full result)
curl -fsS -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8765/redact \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"text":"Email alice@example.com or call 555-123-4567"}' | jq .

# Single redact (text only)
curl -fsS -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8765/redact/text \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"text":"Alice was born 1990-01-02"}'

# Batch redact
curl -fsS -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8765/redact/batch \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"texts":["Hello world","alice@example.com","555-123-4567"]}' | jq .
```

### Python usage

```python
import httpx

# Single redact
resp = httpx.post(
    "http://127.0.0.1:8765/redact",
    json={"text": "Contact alice@example.com"},
)
result = resp.json()
print(result["redacted_text"])  # "Contact <PRIVATE_EMAIL>"
print(result["summary"]["span_count"])  # 1

# Text-only redact
resp = httpx.post(
    "http://127.0.0.1:8765/redact/text",
    json={"text": "Alice email is alice@example.com"},
)
print(resp.text)  # "<PRIVATE_PERSON> email is <PRIVATE_EMAIL>"

# Batch redact
resp = httpx.post(
    "http://127.0.0.1:8765/redact/batch",
    json={"texts": ["Hello", "alice@example.com"]},
)
results = resp.json()
for r in results:
    print(r["text"], "->", r["redacted_text"])
```
